programme.title: What If
programme.desc: Take an epic journey through hypothetical worlds and possibilities.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Chaleur en classe : devrait-on prévoir des journées de « congé canicule »?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Dr Alain Vadeboncoeur, Maude Goyer et notre invitée la chroniqueuse en éducation Claudine Potvin.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre dévoiler l'identité des députés qui auraient contribué à de l'ingérence étrangère?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Antonine Yaccarini, Me Nada Boumeftah et Rodolphe Husny.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Doit-on imposer des peines plus sévères aux mineurs coupables de crime?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Rodolphe Husny, Déborah Cherenfant, Victor Henriquez et notre invitée l'avocate criminaliste Me Elfriede Duclervil.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre amener un enfant en bas âge à de grands spectacles et événements?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Alex Perron, Geneviève Pettersen, François Lambert et Catherine Beauchamp.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre la consommation d'alcool dans les parcs?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Maude Goyer, François Lambert, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Déborah Cherenfant et Meeker Guerrier.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre les élus qui bloquent les commentaires sur les réseaux sociaux?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Rodolphe Husny, Maude Goyer, Jacinthe-Eve Arel et Victor Henriquez.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Qui sortira gagnant du débat présidentiel Trump-Harris?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Victor Henriquez, Valérie Beaudoin et Rodolphe Husny.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Sentiment d'insécurité dans nos villes : pour ou contre le port du poivre de cayenne?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, Antonine Yaccarini, Me Nada Boumeftah et Déborah Cherenfant.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre les toilettes non genrées?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Maude Goyer, Catherine Beauchamp et notre invité l'influenceur PL Cloutier.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre des élections fédérales avant les Fêtes?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Maude Goyer, François Lambert et l'analyste politique et ancien stratège du NPD Karl Bélanger.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Est-ce au gouvernement d'encadrer la pratique des pourboires?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Luc Ferrandez, Maude Goyer, Catherine Beauchamp et le propriétaire de la brasserie Le Saint-Bock, Martin Guimond.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: L'intelligence artificielle est-elle une menace pour notre sécurité?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Me Nada Boumeftah et nos invités le journaliste Alain McKenna et le chroniqueur techno François Charron.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: La RAMQ devrait-elle rembourser les médicaments pour la perte de poids?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Geneviève Pettersen, Jacinthe-Eve Arel et l'invitée l'omnipraticienne retraitée Dre Christiane Laberge.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre les hypothèques sur 30 ans pour aider l'accès à la propriété?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Rodolphe Husny, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, le comptable Pierre-Yves McSween et le courtier immobilier Younes El-Moustir.

programme.title: Fire Down Below
programme.desc: A pair of small-time smugglers have a falling out over the love of a sexy, and disreputable woman.

programme.title: The Amazing Mr. Williams
programme.desc: A crafty detective courts trouble as he sets out to clear the name of a suspected murderer.

programme.title: How To Save A Marriage (And Ruin Your Life)
programme.desc: To save his best friend's marriage, a lawyer seduces the man's mistress, not realizing it is the wrong woman.

programme.title: It Happened One Night
programme.desc: A heiress falls in love with the newspaperman she enlists to help her escape her father.

programme.title: The Long Walk Home
programme.desc: One black and one white woman in 1955 Montgomery, Alabama must decide what they are going to do in response to the famous bus boycott lead by Martin Luther King.

programme.title: Brigsby Bear
programme.desc: The show Brigsby Bear Adventures is a children's television program produced for an audience of one: James Pope. When the series abruptly ends, Pope's life changes forever as he sets out to finish the storyline himself. To do that, he must learn how to cope with the realities of a new world that he knows nothing about.

programme.title: Scent of Murder
programme.desc: Sherilyn Fenn ("Twin Peaks") stars as Brenna, the leader of a search-and-rescue dog team who becomes the focus of a kidnapper after authorities begin to unravel his dangerous games.

programme.title: My Other Mother
programme.desc: When gruff and independent Candy meets her stubborn birth mother, will the two manage to open their hearts?

programme.title: Rough Air: Danger on Flight 534
programme.desc: A reluctant co-pilot is forced to take command of a passenger plane after the captain is severely injured.

programme.title: Mazes and Monsters
programme.desc: A fantasy medieval game turns deadly when Robbie (Tom Hanks) and his friends take it into the real world.

programme.title: Rush of Fear
programme.desc: Rosanna Arquette and Chris Potter star as couple Alex and Jack, who are away on a romantic weekend to try to salvage their broken marriage. Their trip turns into a nightmare when Jack is kidnapped.

programme.title: Point Blank
programme.desc: Mickey Rourke and Danny Trejo star in this action-packed drama about a gang of psychotic criminals who break out of prison and take control of a Texas shopping mall.

programme.title: La correspondance du coeur
programme.desc: Cinq ans après une rencontre marquante avec une inconnue, un photographe la retrouve dans un avion qui les conduit au mariage de celle-ci.

programme.title: Handcrafted America
programme.desc: Handcrafted America - Pianos, Cowboy Hats, Tin Coffee Pots: Host Jill Wagner travels the country on a quest to find talented craftsmen who still make incredible items by hand. The town of Cave Creek, Arizona is a nostalgic tribute to the Old West. That's where Jill finds cowboy hat maker Eric Watson, and learns why cowboys prefer his custom hats. In Freeport, Maine, Jill meets Rodney Regier, a piano maker who has a passion for making old-fashioned wood pianos. Finally, in Janesville, Wisconsin, Jill shares a cup of coffee with tinsmith Jim Feavel, who shows her how he makes authentic 19th century coffee pots and mugs.

programme.title: Handcrafted America
programme.desc: Handcrafted America - Brooms, Copper Kitchenware, Hawaiian Drums: Host Jill Wagner travels the country on a quest to find talented craftsmen who still make incredible items by hand. In Mountain View, Arkansas, Jill cleans up with Jerry Lovenstein, who combines dried broomcorn with wood he finds on his land and makes beautiful handcrafted brooms. Next, in Nashville, Tennessee, Ben Caldwell shows Jill how he creates interesting, sturdy kitchenware using copper and deer antler sheds. Finally, in Hilo on the big island of Hawaii, Jill visits Keoni Turalde. He carves ornate, traditional Pahu drums from a coconut tree using only a chisel, a gouge and a lot of patience.

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.desc: Into The Wild Frontier - Andrew Henry: Man of His Word: Though Andrew Henry has little experience when he partners in a trapping expedition, he pledges his word to complete the risky venture. As Blackfeet warriors close in, he must weigh his personal integrity against the lives of the men he leads.

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.desc: Into The Wild Frontier - William Bartram: America's First Great Naturalist: Naturalist William Bartram explores the unmapped regions of the South to document the native fauna and flora. After surviving many encounters with Native Americans and wildlife, it's an invisible virus that may finally end the journey and his life.

Schmidt) and Joey (Gino Calabro) in the middle of the desert.

programme.title: Playing the Role
programme.desc: First, Lou devises a plan to have Froy and Sonia pose as house buyers, and a happy married couple, meanwhile Matt and Lou will hook and book. Then, Matt and Froy wrangle with an out-of-work actor who's lost track of the line between his identity and his latest role. Finally, Froy and Lyndah need to figure out how to get a car from an angry owner, and the competing repo company that got to the job first.

programme.title: Honor Rolling Away
programme.desc: Sonia and Lyndah find themselves at a local University trying to reclaim a car from a not-so-honorable honor roll student with a squeaky voice. Then, Matt and Sonia are tasked with snagging a tricked-out race car from two vested thugs armed with golf clubs. Finally, Lou and Sonia head to a fancy part of town to repo an Escalade from a young professional and his unsupportive wife.

programme.title: Look What You Did!
programme.desc: First, Sonia and Froy get locked in the battle of a lifetime with a pair of young debtors who are understandably upset that their late model Tahoe has found its way to the bottom of a lake. Is it Froy's fault, as they claim, or are there other forces at work here? Then, Lou and Matt get down and dirty with a taser-packing P.I. who isn't ready to say goodbye to his ride. Finally, when Frankie puts the go-jacks on the wrong car, it plucks Matt's last nerve and places him at risk for the beat-down of the century.

programme.title: Pepper Spray and Guns
programme.desc: First, Froy and Sonia douse a couple with a dose of pepper spray when the debtor gets too pushy with Sonia. Next, Matt and Froy battle biker girls when going after a motorcycle. And Finally, Matt and Froy confront a couple of delinquents in attempt to repo the couple's car, but they are soon sent running when the couple pushes back with guns.

programme.title: Think, Adapt and Attack
programme.desc: When a debtor and his friends resort to baseball bats, chaos erupts, and Lou, Lyndah and Sonia fight not only with them, but also with each other. Down in Texas, Matt and Froy wait to clear up Froy's warrant and get in way over their heads working for a local repo team. Meanwhile, Lou decides to re-hire Frankie despite Sonia and Lyndah's objections, but first Lou wants to see how Frankie fares when faced with the temptation in the form of strippers and alcohol.

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E01
programme.desc: In this episode, we head straight into the heart of New Zealand! We begin back in time, in an era of proud warriors and ancient traditions with Tamaki Maori Village, the most award-winning cultural attraction in New Zealand. Then we step through Hell's Gate and experience the country's most unique geothermal occurrence. Eager to catch our breath, we lose it walking amongst the breathing majestic giants at Redwood Tree Walk. And finally, arrive in Hobbiton. Yes, that Hobbiton of lore!

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E02
programme.desc: In this episode, we seek refuge in Waiheke, New Zealand. Stonyridge Vineyards, a farm that is less about cropping and all about farming flavor- proves to be a fine locality from which to begin this episodic excursion into paradise. Unsurprisingly, our appetite for ingesting the best of this region leads us to the nutrient-rich, Te Matuku Marine Reserve- where we learn the value of a keystone species of the ecosystem... OYSTERS! Bellyful and bound to take a dip, we meander to Waiheke Island Resor

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E03
programme.desc: In this episode, we admire history from up high while visiting the oldest surviving lighthouse on mainland Australia. Then, we enter into a passionate relationship between an unordinary couple that involves alpacas. Next, our own expectations are blown away by retreating at the 12 Apostles/Alkina Lodge! Following that, we find a soulmate to our fellow animals at Great Ocean Road Wildlife Park. And finally, take a seat at the comfortable whiskey bar at Timboon Distillery before walking on top of

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E04
programme.desc: In this episode, we arrive in Sydney by departing into the Ken Done realm of artistry. Set a spin, we then roam the regions of Coffs Harbour and Urunga! Partaking in a happiness revolution in the shape of a grocery store (The Happy Frog) to paddle-boarding with direct descendants of the world's first stand up paddlers down a historical stream- this episode highlights the aim of being open to diversity in epic forms!

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E05
programme.desc: In this episode, we "hang" with some friends abseiling over Southbank Parklands! Then we gather our courage and ascend the Story Bridge. Next, we mount some horses and find ourselves prospecting for gemstones at Thunderbird Park. Finally, we pull over and unwind in koala territory up on Mount Tambourine.

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E06
programme.desc: In this episode, we discover where Australia begins by embarking down the Mulgrave River on an exclusive day tour out to the Frankland Islands. Next, we explore a bit of cultural history by spearfishing alongside the Daintree Rainforest. Then we go full eco-touristic while visiting a revered sanctuary of wildlife, Hartley's Crocodile Adventure! Ultimately, we arrive at Fitzroy Island Retreat. Enough said!

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E07
programme.desc: In this episode, we pursue the spirit of ambition while discovering Hawaii's immortal culture! From walking the hallowed grounds of Hilo Coffee Mill to witnessing the reinvention of traditions at Old Lahaina Luau- this episode represents the best of what is to be expected while traveling wide-eyed through paradise!

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E08
programme.desc: In this episode, we visit The Island of Hawaii better known as “The Big Island.” Here, we explore the history and geography of the islands, learn how to Hula Dance, visit a tranquil secluded retreat, learn the art of lei-making, and have some sea and land adventures!

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E09
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the taste of pacific cuisine. From Hawaiian fusion and the beloved coffee bean to New Zealand oysters and tropical fish, Didiayer shares a variety of tastes around the pacific rim.

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E10
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore culture old and new through a variety of unique destinations. Whether it be doing the Haka with a Maori tribe, weaving alpaca hair, or getting the real story behind the Hawaiian Luau, this episode dives in to the customs that bring people together.

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E11
programme.desc: In this episode, we step out and in to nature to explore what adventure awaits. Amongst rappelling, zip-lining, horseback riding, snorkeling with dolphins and feeding crocodiles there is hardly a moment to catch your breath!

programme.title: Travel with Didiayer S03 E12
programme.desc: In this episode, we share some of the best places from our journey where we chose to rest our heads. High-class or just like home, these lovely accommodations are sure to fit anyone's budget and style.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | Broncos, Galaxies and a lifted Grand Wagoneer
programme.desc: Tom Cotter finds all sorts of interesting vehicles on a trip to Northern Michigan, from Ford Broncos to a unique Ford Galaxie. Speaking of Galaxies, hang around for a look at a Galaxie 500 that is back on the road after being a "barn find" itself!

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | Millon-dollar Barn Find!
programme.desc: Tom Cotter helps "unearth" a 1952 Pegaso Z102, one of only 85 made, from a California garage. Next, we find Tom in South Carolina where he finds a plethora of American muscle cars, including a one-owner Plymouth Cuda.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | Lamborghini kit car and basement full of cool cars
programme.desc: Tom Cotter finds some interesting cars in the basement of a large house in a residential neighborhood in Southern Utah. Next, Tom is in Georgia where finds a 1965 Ford Fairlane with a 289 V-8 and 4-speed manual transmission.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | Private tour of Collier Motors, an abandoned car dealership
programme.desc: Tom Cotter gets a private tour of Collier Motors, an AMC dealership that was abandoned 40 years ago. Next, Tom is in Maine where he finds an LS-swapped Rolls Royce, Plymouth Barracuda, and a unique Harley Davidson.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | Rare Lotus Eleven, a 327 Corvette Stingray, a Chevy C10 with 148 original miles
programme.desc: This episode features a rare Lotus Eleven once owned by Colin Chapman, a 327 Chevy Corvette Stingray with a 4-speed, and a Chevrolet C10 pickup truck with only 148 miles on the odometer.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | Classic Fords, Chevys and some dusty Italian sports cars!
programme.desc: Tom Cotter finds himself in Michigan again where he comes upon a pole barn full of Chevys and Fords that owner Denny has all sorts of stories about. Tom then heads to the other end of the state for some European sports car barn finds.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | R-Code Ford, De Tomaso Pantera, and Ford Hot Rods
programme.desc: Tom Cotter finds an R-Code Ford Galaxie 500, a De Tomaso Pantera and a more. Next, he heads to Alaska and discovers a stash of Ford hot rods.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | 1,800 cars in remote area of California
programme.desc: Tom Cotter heads to the "middle of nowhere" in rural California where a unique collection of 1,800 classic cars is slowly returning to the earth. Next, we find Tom in the Carolinas where he discovers a 1954 Chevy Corvette and so much more.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | Massive stash of cars in old car dealership
programme.desc: Tom Cotter gets a tour of an enormous "museum" of forgotten cars stashed in a defunct car dealership. Tom's next stop reveals a rare Corvair, a large collection of Mazdas, and a rare "Alfa Jr."

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | Lifetime collection of old Fords & rare motorcycles
programme.desc: Tom Cotter reunites with an old friend from his NASCAR days and gets a tour of his collection of old Fords and rare motorcycles. After that, Tom uncovers an old Corvette and a handful of iconic British sports cars.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter | Unimogs, Mercedes 190E 2.3-16 and starting up a barn find
programme.desc: Tom Cotter meets a long-time Mercedes mechanic and tours his collection of Unimogs and turbo diesel old Mercs. Next, Tom in is in the UK where he and a friend attempt to start a Morris Minor that hasn't run in over 20 years.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience

programme.title: Kim's Convenience

programme.title: Kim's Convenience

programme.title: Kim's Convenience

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: Appa inherits Janet's old smartphone. Umma finds a pest in the store. Kimchee tries to help Janet cure her case of the FOMOs, while Jung tries to reconnect with a girl he met at a bar.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.sub-title: The Man with Two Red Shoes
programme.desc: Lisa starts a friendly ‘Teacher of the Month' competition and it's no surprise when Mr. Dwyer wins the first one. Gerry feels slighted and is determined to find a way to earn next month's title. When word gets out that a lurker has been spotted in the neighbourhood, Gerry sets out to catch him. Of course, catching a lurker is no small feat and Gerry must use some rather unconventional tactics if he's to succeed. Meanwhile, Robert has been working on an art installation made up of photos of Xavier's staff and students. Bobbi is upset to find she has seemingly been excluded from the project and confronts him about his unresolved feelings for her. At the same time, an unexpected feud develops between Dwyer and Nisha when they discover that they've been using the same catchphrase. After all, “Every day's a holiday” can only belong to one.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry's blown away when he lays eyes on the new hot substitute teacher, Mary. Conveniently forgetting that he's technically married, he vows to woo her. But to his shock and horror, Gerry sees a second hot sub, Shane, who appears to be his fiercest competitor for Mary's affection. Gerry tasks Simon with keep hot sub Shane away from hot sub Mary, until Gerry can lock that down. Simon assures Gerry that he's on it, but finds the mission interfering with his five-month anniversary plans with Nisha. Bobbi has been having trouble with foul-mouthed and disruptive Alex. Taking Dwyer's advice, she sets out to change his attitude through positive reinforcement.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry walks in on Trudy and Emma watching a viral video of a bus driver being bullied by students. After learning about a crowdfunding effort that raised money for the poor bus driver, he recruits Simon to help him stage a ‘bullying' incident on the bus, in which he is to be the sad victim. Meanwhile, Bobbi takes a dozen pregnancy tests and finally accepts that she's pregnant. When the tests are discovered, she finds herself roped into Robert's misguided hunt for twelve frightened pregnant young students.. Dwyer has a new lady in his life and seems happier than he's been in a long time. Nisha couldn't be more pleased for him, until she discovers that his new love interest bears a striking resemblance to the sketch composite of the suspect in his late wife's murder case.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: It's almost the end of the school year, and Gerry seems to be the only teacher at Xavier who hasn't been offered a full-time contract for next fall. Even after Gerry plays the marriage card, it seems Lisa can't guarantee him anything beyond fulltime substitute. Unwilling to accept this fate, Gerry gets an idea to start his own Charter school. Meanwhile, Robert and Bobbi interview applicants to fill Bobbi's position while she's away on maternity leave. Her initial concern about finding a qualified candidate is replaced with a deep insecurity that she won't even be missed when the candidates prove quite overqualified. Lisa pulls out all the stops to throw an end-of-year faculty party. But when her efforts seem to go unappreciated, Lisa makes a bold move.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.sub-title: Gerry Wants to Make Phys Ed Great Again
programme.desc: It's a new school year and Gerry has finally achieved his goal: full-time Phys Ed! But when he finds out how Bobbi's been teaching Phys Ed, Gerry's appalled, and sets out to teach the course the way it should be taught…or the way he thinks it should be taught, anyways. Meanwhile, Dwyer learns the ropes to his newly appointed position of Vice Principal and Nisha, fresh off her camp counsellor summer job, struggles to reset boundaries with three seniors.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Lisa, now married to Alex's father and still reeling from her Xavier departure, sets out to punish the staff by running for the unopposed position of PTA president. Fearing their Lisa-free days may be numbered, Gerry and Robert go to great lengths to block her candidacy. Meanwhile, Malik isn't overly fond of his new job, and Simon and Nisha may be a little too fond of each other…in the school.

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: Kate's mother, who is not on warm terms with Willie, arrives for a surprise visit that forces the Tanners to lock ALF in the garage for "a few days". The problem is that ALF just can't stand being not allowed to roam around in the house, with all the luxuries found there.

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: ALF and Kate's mom are arguing about TV. Kate's mom thinks ALF's favorite soap opera, Midwest General, is sleaze, and shows him her favorite, One World to Hope For, which ALF thinks is boring. Angered, ALF argues that he could write a better script for the series than the writers are coming up with.

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: Willie catches ALF from eating a box of chocolate that was to be a Valentine's Day present for Kate. This incites Willie to give ALF a lecture on how ALF does not respect private property and needs to adopt a new attitude. Also, Kate's mom is moving out of the Tanners' house.

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: ALF bumps his head and loses his memory. All he knows is that his name is Wayne Schlagel and that he's an insurance agent. The Tanners try to make ALF remember who he really is.

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: ALF is now into gardening, which means he's turned over the entire Tanner backyard. Willie has a problem at work: a lost Mexican boy is at his office and Willie is trying to track down his father. When that fails, Willie has no option but to bring the boy home.

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: ALF starts to go through a phase where his erratic behavior and hunger for cats will, in his own words, make him "ALF to the tenth power". He wants Willie to build a cage where he can be locked in until the phase is over. Will the cage be strong enough?

programme.title: ALF
programme.sub-title: Going Out of My Head Over You
programme.desc: Brian has to lie to his friend who wants to come for a sleepover that the Tanners' basement is flooded, because ALF must be kept secret. Willie then tries to explain to Brian that lying is always wrong even though Brian has to keep lying because of ALF.

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: There's a power blackout, so ALF is passing the time by watching the Tanners' neighbors, the Ochmoneks, who are fighting. By dinner time, the power has come back so the Tanners can have dinner. Then comes another, short blackout. Trevor Ochmonek appears at the Tanners' door...

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: Lynn has introduced ALF to rap music, much to Willie's dislike. Willie himself is making the final changes to Brian's song in the school pageant where the kids dress as vegetables. Unfortunately, Brian is suffering from stage fright, which causes him to forget the lyrics. ALF offers to help...

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: The Tanners are preparing for a garage sale to get some money for house payments. ALF discovers the alluring world of horse racing thanks to Kate's mom who bets on them. When the concept of a bookie is explained to ALF, he gets the bright idea of getting the Tanners some money through betting.

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: When the living room TV breaks down, Willie declares that he won't have it fixed, despite ALF's complaints that he has nothing to do but watch TV. ALF keeps pressuring Willie, who discovers that the TV is still under warranty and he can have it sent to the factory in Libya...

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: An insect from Melmac has an unexpected reaction when exposed to earthly chemicals.

programme.title: ALF

programme.title: ALF
programme.sub-title: Working My Way Back to You

programme.title: Angry Planet | Wild Water
programme.desc: Three explorations into the wild water that makes up over 70% of our angry planet. First, George travels to Guadaloupe Island off the coast of Mexico to dive with 16 foot Great White Sharks. He then uses drysuit and kayak to retrace the explorations of Tom Woodward into a flooded Northern Ontario cave in the middle of winter, then jetboats into the world's biggest Class VI whitewater rapids in the Niagara River.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Boiling Lake
programme.desc: Dominica, the most volcanic and mountainous island in the Caribbean, is home to the Boiling Lake, a large waterfall-fed lake of furiously boiling water. Adventurer George Kourounis becomes the first person ever to traverse across the lake on a rope, risking his life to take water samples and shoot video from a place no human has ever been. He then dives to the ocean floor off the coast of the island to explore vents where the superheated water bubbles into the ocean.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Blizzard
programme.desc: Mount Washington, New Hampshire has the dubious title of being home to the world's worst weather. The highest wind ever recorded on the planet – 231 miles per hour – was recorded at its mountaintop observatory. To prepare for a mid-winter assault on the summit, Angry Planet host George Kourounis becomes a “human popsicle”, encased in ice in at the National Resarch Council's high speed wind tunnel. Then he and his sidekick Mark Robinson climb to the top of the mountain in mid-January and brave their way into a summit blizzard.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Volcanic Wedding
programme.desc: Host George Kourounis has braved many terrifying phenomena – but none as frightening as the one he takes on in this episode: marriage! To make sure his new bride is on the same adventurous page with him, he proposes to get married on the lip of an exploding volcano, on the South Pacific island of Tanna, in Vanuatu. Before the native preacher, dressed in grass skirt and kustom garb, gets to perform the smoky vows, George descends into the erupting volcano to bring out red hot samples of the lava it is producing.

programme.title: Fight To Survive | Lightning Strike
programme.desc: A bass fishing trip goes horribly wrong when a bolt of lightning strikes their boat, leaving Seventeen-year-old Christian Neal burned and unconscious and his father Gabe blown into the water and paralyzed. It will take a miracle to save them.

programme.title: Fight To Survive | Ken Henderson Story
programme.desc: Best friends, Ed Cohen and Ken Henderson, set out for two days of Texas gulf fishing. Tragedy strikes and the two men are stranded adrift in hostile waters.

programme.title: Fight To Survive | Stranded in the Alaska Wilderness Part 2
programme.desc: After a successful hunt., Adrian Knopps' hunting partner disappears taking their bear out to the boat. Now Adrian finds himself stranded on a tidal flat surround by bears and wolf packs and with massive storm bearing down on him. How will he survive?

programme.title: Fight To Survive | A River's Grasp
programme.desc: In an isolated mountain river, Fisherman Dean Ririe becomes hopelessly trapped under a boulder in water up to his neck. In icy waters that should kill a man, Dean fights for his life miles from help.

programme.title: Fight To Survive | Nightmare Canyon Pt. 2
programme.desc: Justin and Jeremy Harris&apos weekend descent into a remote Utah canyon becomes a survival nightmare when Justin severely breaks his leg. As night fast approaches and temperatures drop below freezing, how will these two brothers survive?

programme.title: Fight To Survive | Stranded in the Alaska Wilderness Part 1
programme.desc: Hunters Adrian Knopps and Garrett Hagen venture into the remotest part of Alaska's Misty Fjord, hunting Moose and Bear. After a successful hunt Adrian finds himself trapped by a twenty foot tidal surge after Garrett fails to return with the skiff.

programme.title: Fight To Survive | Adrift Part 2
programme.desc: Facing starvation, dehydration and predators from the deep, sailor Steven Callahan fights to survive not only the physical hardships, but also the mental and emotional strain of being adrift and alone in a disintegrating life raft.

programme.title: Fight To Survive | Under the Desert Sun
programme.desc: Ed Rosenthal set out for a short hike in Joshua Tree National Park, but in the heat of the day he became turned around and lost. His six-day ordeal of survival in blazing temperatures with no food or water is nothing short of miraculous.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Wordsworth: First Words Around the House
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Einstein: Let's Learn Signs: Around Town
programme.desc: Discover sign language! Enjoy upbeat, classical melodies as you learn how to sign words and activities related to common main street businesses, where your food comes from, activities related to dining out and more. Learn through real-life footage, narration, and repetition.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Baby Lullaby takes your baby on a soothing journey with classical lullabies by Bach, Brahms and Mozart. Enjoy relaxing ocean waves, sweet puppet shows and tranquil images. From fun time to rest time, Baby Lullaby is the perfect way to spend special time with your baby.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: As little ones become better acquainted with the world, they grow curious about animals of all kinds, beyond just the family pet. And as their communication skills continue to develop, they enjoy imitating animal sounds as well. Baby Noah Animal Expedition takes your little explorer across the globe to meet animals -- two-by-two -- from the savannah, the rainforest and tropics, the Outback, the ocean and the Polar Regions.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby MacDonald: A Day On the Farm
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Your little one will want to keep a beat, snap his fingers and march his feet! Join new characters Pillie, the musical woodpecker, and Coco, the dancing cockatoo, and share the excitement of rhythm with your baby. World of Rhythm explores rhythm in music, rhythm in nature and rhythm as a form of expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: As little ones grow, their innate sense of wonder grows too, creating an occasion for celebration with each new discovery. Baby Galileo Discovering the Sky introduces them to that remarkable canopy above us: the sun and moon, fluffy clouds, shimmering stars, colorful planets and whirling galaxies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Share a rainbow of sights and sounds with your baby as you discover the wonders of color together! Join puppet host "Vincent Van Goat" as he creates six Van Gogh masterpieces exploring red, green, blue, yellow, orange, and purple. World Of Colors is a spirited celebration of creative expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Einstein: Let's Learn about Classical Music
programme.desc: Discover instruments through narration and repetition. Real-life images and sounds will engage viewers as they dance to upbeat classical favorites from Beethoven, Mozart and more. Join your favorite Baby Einstein characters to discover what makes each instrument unique!

programme.desc: When the girls discover that Apple's groove booties can teach the pups to dance, Plum gets an idea for a Doggie Dance Show. Wanting to impress them, Huck offers to have TomTom join the show... despite the pup's obvious dislike of performing in public. It's up to Strawberry to help Huck see that he should not force TomTom into something he just doesn't want to do.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Sour wishes she could have a birthday all her very own, her twin sister Sweet grants her wish by forgoing celebrating her birthday. But what begins as a day that Sour has always dreamed of quickly becomes a disappointment when she misses her twin. With Strawberry's help, Sour gives her birthday back to Sweet with a special surprise.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When a simple tune Cherry composes goes viral on the Berryweb, Cherry quickly gets fed up with everyone listening to this song, everywhere, all the time. She goes to comic lengths to get some peace so she can compose the song she's suppose to write for the Queen of Berryvania.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Berry Bitty City is holding its first city-wide talent show! Hearing that it's the music students of superstar Cherry Jam, a talent scout from Big Bitty City plans to show up. Strawberry and her friends excitedly anticipate her arrival.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Sour wishes she could have a birthday all her very own, her twin sister Sweet grants her wish by forgoing celebrating her birthday. But what begins as a day that Sour has always dreamed of quickly becomes a disappointment when she misses her twin. With Strawberry's help, Sour gives her birthday back to Sweet with a special surprise.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's time again for the Dance-Off between the Big Bitty City Dance Team and the Berry Bitty City Dancers. Plum is stressed out and frazzled because she desperately wants to win. And the Big Bitty City Dancers are a force to be reckoned with. They are elegant and beautifully synchronized. Plum shows the girls a maze of dance steps that she's choreographed in the hopes of creating a winning piece. The problem is, the contest is only 1 week away!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Apple feels left out of a Berryball game and an advanced ballet class, she invents some "groove booties" programmed to dance to any beat. She temporarily impresses her friends with her dance moves, but when the groove booties go out of control and threaten the town's Berryball game, she is forced to fess up.

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: The Berry Big Harvest
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store. Soon Orange's store is overflowing with Berry Bitty Fruits & Veggies. It's the berry first time Orange has not been able to find a “place for everything” in her store.

programme.title: Playback
programme.desc: Ghost Girl, a bounty hunter who works for SOL Technologies, holds an interview with two reporters recounting all the events that have transpired concerning the evil Knights of Hanoi and the mysterious Playmaker.

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: An Invitation
programme.desc: Ghost Girl challenges Yusaku to a Speed Duel with some heavy stakes. If she wins, she gets to capture Ai. If Yusaku wins, he gets a window into SOL's server to learn more about the incident that still haunts him.

programme.title: Blippi The Musical
programme.desc: Blippi The Musical brings the lively Blippi from the screen to the stage in an exciting live show. Recorded in Nashville, TN, the performance features 14 songs, including favorites like "Do the Blippi Dance," "Brush Brush Your Teeth," and "Monster Truck." Your Blippi fan will enjoy learning about the world while singing and dancing along with this special show. There's so much to explore—it'll make you want to sing and shout!

programme.title: Room at the Top
programme.desc: Strawberry is really touched when her girlfriends and the Berrykins volunteer to help her with the construction of her new marketplace, so when the girls suggest they take charge of designing Strawberry's dream-bedroom to go on the second story, of course Strawberry agrees. While construction is going on, the girls each show Strawberry their individual ideas for the bedroom. Grateful for the support and not wanting to dampen anyone's enthusiasm, Strawberry tells each of them yes (actually, more like not-no).

programme.title: Starlight, Star Bright
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine! The girls and Berrykins rush to turn the Marketplace Café into a suitable performance area for a big star like Cherry Jam.

programme.title: Blippi's Alphabet and Transportation Adventures
programme.desc: Join Blippi on a series of exciting educational journeys where he uncovers the Case of the Missing ABCs, explores the charming Ranch at the Pier, and delves into London's rich transportation history at both the Transport Museum and the RAF Museum. From solving puzzles and meeting farm animals to discovering vintage buses and historic airplanes, Blippi makes learning an adventure filled with curiosity and fun.

programme.title: Blippi's Fun and Colorful Exploration
programme.desc: Get ready for a vibrant and entertaining escapade with Blippi as he dives into a world of colors at Billy Beez, creates a whimsical pizza in the kitchen, transforms into a playful pirate on a treasure hunt, and explores the fascinating world of dinosaurs at the Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History. Each adventure is packed with laughter, creativity, and educational moments, making learning a joyful experience for kids.

programme.title: The Berry Big Harvest
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store. Soon Orange's store is overflowing with Berry Bitty Fruits & Veggies. It's the berry first time Orange has not been able to find a “place for everything” in her store.

programme.title: All My Senses
programme.desc: Sid wants to know about all his different senses. At school, Sid and his friends embark on a quest to explore vision, hearing, touch, smell, and taste!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh!
programme.desc: Yu-Gi-Oh! - The Dark Spirit Revealed: Part 2: Bakura activates Destiny Board, which will cause Yugi to lose automatically in five turns. If that's not bad enough?

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh!
programme.desc: Yu-Gi-Oh! - The Dark Spirit Revealed: Part 3: Yugi's Slifer the Sky Dragon is about to unleash the final blow to win the duel, but Bakura may not survive the blast from the almighty Egyptian God Monster. Will Yugi attack and advance in the finals or will he surrender to save Bakura's life?

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - No Body Knows: Lord Betrayus steals the Repository of the ghosts' corporeal bodies, but not before Inky, Blinky, Pinky and Clyde nab one body to share between them.

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - Seems Like Old Times: Sir Cumference tries to show that he is more than just an old scientist, he's still got it as a freedom fighter - but after some disastrous attempts, it soon becomes clear that he isn't quite the fighter he used to be.

programme.title: Skylanders Academy
programme.desc: Skylanders Academy - Raiders of the Lost Arkus, Part 1: In order to beat Strykore to Arkus, the Skylanders hatch a dangerous plan to rescue Cynder from the clutches of her evil father, Malefor.

programme.title: Skylanders Academy
programme.desc: Skylanders Academy - Raiders of the Lost Arkus, Part 2: It's all-out war as the Skylanders try to stop Kaos from destroying the Core of Light.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3
programme.desc: Super Mario Bros 3 - Toadally Magical Adventure: Toad loses a magic wand to the Koopas. / When the Mario Brothers go back to Brooklyn, they are mistaken as babysitters to look after Junior. Mario & Luigi must control and get Junior back home.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3
programme.desc: Super Mario Bros 3 - Oh Brother / Mighty Plumber: When Mario and Luigi get fed up with each other, Luigi soon finds himself having to save his brother from Koopa and Kooky. / Koopa brings TV hero Mighty Plumber to life and tricks him into robbing the Pipe Land Treasury.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh!
programme.desc: Yu-Gi-Oh! - Rage of the Egyptian Gods: The origin of the Egyptian God Cards is finally revealed. The mysterious Shadi takes Yugi on a journey back in time to the creation of Duel Monsters… and the dangerous powers it released.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh!
programme.desc: Yu-Gi-Oh! - Awakening of Evil Part 1: In the second duel of the Battle City finals, Odion, disguised as Marik, befuddles Joey with numerous traps to bring the duel to a stalemate.

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - Betrayus Turns the Heat Up: PacWorld is becoming dangerously hot and its citizens mistakenly blame Sir C!

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - Pac-Pong Fever: The annual Pacolympics approach and allow for Aunt Spheria to reignite a Pac-Pong rivalry with her former Pac-Pong rival and wanna-be suitor - Lord Betrayus.

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - Indiana Pac and the Temple of Slime: An ancient artifact once belonging to Pac's parent's leads to a temple within a maze that may hold the secret to the Tree of Life.

programme.title: All Of Nothing
programme.desc: The ninja and Sensei Wu try to infiltrate the Serpentines' inner sanctum and walk right into a trap, and when Lloyd's rescue mission fails, there is only one person who can help them, namely the evil Lord Garmadon – but can they trust him?

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes: Maximum Overload
programme.desc: Spider-Man and Marvel's Super Heroes take on a mischievous Loki and a team of super villains in an all-new LEGO adventure

programme.title: Back To School
programme.desc: When the NEXO-Knights are sloppy in a fight with Jestro, Merlok 2.0 sends them back to the Knights' Academy. Not only for a brush-up but to find out what Jestro is after in its hallowed halls. As they Knights battle Jestro – in a somewhat haphazard and lazy way – they discuss the fact that Jestro never wins and that they're always getting over on him. Despite defeating the jerky Jester and his evil Book, Merlok 2.0 discovers that something about the attack on the village is tied to the Knights' Academy. It looks like that's where they'll strike next. It wouldn't hurt for the Knights to go “back to school” to watch for Jestro and for some refresher training. Merlok is sure Jestro wants something at the Academy, but he doesn't know what it is. The Knights are very much “fish out of water” at the academy, even with the “cool kids” – Robin and Ava – showing them around. The next generation of Knights is being taught to integrate the NEXO-Magic of Merlok into their fighting techniques and these kids are good (so good, they initially kick the lazy, know-it-all Knight's butts!) Their old principal, the ever-ornery Headmaster Brickland, sets them straight: they're all still slackers, and they better get their acts together (especially Lance!) The Knights take shifts as “library helpers” much to the delight of the old squirebot librarian, MARGE. They're convinced Jestro is after a book…isn't he always after a book? When Jestro attacks, the Knights help organize the students and there's a big fight in the Academy Library versus Jestro and his Monsters. They drive Jestro off without letting him steal any books! Victory! But as Jestro leaves, we see him holding what the Book of Monsters really wanted: a snow globe with a castle in it that was sitting on the librarian's desk. In the Lava Lands, Jestro throws the snow globe into a volcano and it frees the massive Castle of Monstrux from its snowy magic prison

programme.title: The CHI Jackers
programme.desc: The Wolves have become strong because of the stolen CHI. And now they are stealing even more. Soon all the other tribes of CHIMA have none. Laval and Eris must find a new, clever and “Wolf proof ” way to get CHI to the tribes.

programme.title: Symptoms!
programme.desc: Owen falls prey to the toxin of a poisonous prehistoric jungle flower and believes he's a raptor.

programme.title: Snow Way
programme.desc: Practicing for the local snow lodge's big snowboard race, Mia has an accident and hurts her ankle. She insists it's no biggie, but when she tries to resume practice, the normally fearless Mia finds she's now terrified of snowboarding! At the local snow lodge's Snowboard Meet, ace snowboarder Mia, trying to raise money for a new Heartlake City animal shelter, enters the fiercest race of all – the Boardercross. Then, while practicing, she has an accident and hurts her ankle. She insists it's no big deal, but once she heals and tries to resume practice, the usually fearless Mia finds she's now terrified of snowboarding! With the help of her Friends, she tries hard to overcome her fear, but her nightmarish visions of snowboard monsters and jeering snowmen have her shaking in her bed. She backs out of the race, disappointing her Friends and herself. Finally, when an avalanche traps Fiona, Mia is forced to overcome her fears and race to the rescue.

programme.title: The Shape of Motion
programme.desc: Arin's quest to find his parents takes an unexpected turn when he and the Ninja stumble upon a baby dragon in need.

programme.title: Enter the City of Temples
programme.desc: Breaking into the heavily guarded City of Temples, they must prove their worth to secure a spot in the tournament.

programme.title: Legacy, Vehicle and Mechs
programme.desc: Zane takes help from Nya for backup memories of their adventures to the Samurai X Cave computer, but Zane encounters a mysterious puzzle buried deep within his code. Nya digitizes Jay and uploads him into Zane's mind. Jay reviews memories of the Ninja's various motorcycles, mechs and planes in order to solve the vehicle-themed puzzle.

programme.title: Spit Take!
programme.desc: A Dilophosaurus runs loose in the park's hotel, and Owen and Claire must find it before the guests notice - and before Vic's dino-hunting security team can damage the hotel and the guests.

programme.title: Grim Vengeance
programme.desc: The Never Witch's ambitions have darkened, leading her to target the Grim Realm. In an unlikely alliance, the dream chasers must join forces with the Nightmare King and his grimspawn minions.

programme.title: Crown Control
programme.desc: The dream chasers stumble upon the fabled Crown of Control with surprising ease, thanks to an unexpected encounter with the King of the Creatures.

programme.title: The Promised Crown Affair
programme.desc: Izzie has vanished into the dream world, and the dream chasers struggle to maintain normalcy in the waking world while secretly searching for her.

programme.title: Friends Reunite
programme.desc: Olly about how his obsession is affecting their relationships. Olly refuses to see the damage he's causing, but it becomes clear that he's lost touch with what really matters.

programme.desc: Kaiba kidnaps Yugi's grandfather to get a rare card.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh
programme.desc: Yugi loses his grandpa's soul to Duel Monsters creator, Maximillion Pegasus.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh
programme.desc: Yugi helps Joey board the boat for Duelist Kingdom to save his sister's eyes.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh
programme.desc: Once Yugi and his friends reach Duelist Kingdom, Pegasus announces that there are many strange secrets on the island Weevil begins to use mysterious new rules against Yugi in a duel and Yugi must figure the rules out and master them to survive.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh
programme.desc: At the Duelist Kingdom, the duel is on Yugi must defeat Weevil Great Moth, which gains power with every turn.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh
programme.desc: Joey undergoes his first duel on the island against Mai Valentine, a professional duelist with an uncanny ability to predict the cards she will draw.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh
programme.desc: Fisherman duelist Mako baits Yugi into a disadvantageous battle.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh
programme.desc: Yugi fights Kaiba's younger brother for control of Kaiba Corporation.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh
programme.desc: Yugi must develop new strategies for battle.

programme.desc: An unseen helper aids Yugi in battle with Seto.

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: After meeting a new Coordinator named Brianna who's a fan of hers and an even bigger fan of Drew's, May discovers she must battle her in order to win her third Pokémon Contest ribbon.

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: After a frustrated Sceptile runs off into the forest, Ash and Pikachu go after it and run into a swarm of angry Beedrill—and the next Frontier Brain!

programme.title: Pokémon

programme.sub-title: Al Jarreau - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. In 1981, American singer and Grammy Award winner Al Jarreau gave an unforgettable performance for the audience of the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague, the Netherlands.

programme.title: Jimmy Smith
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Chet Baker
programme.desc: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential male jazz and pop vocalists in history showcases these legends' unique essence. Delight in the expressive beauty and emotional depth of Chet Baker's rendition of “Time After Time” and “You Don't Know What Love Is", recorded live in Brussels, Belgium in 1964 and in Rome, Italy in 1956 respectively. Revel in Frank Sinatra's iconic version of “I've Got You Under My Skin". Immerse yourself in Dizzy Gillespie's dynamic performance of “Let Me Outta Here”, recorded live in Denmark in 1970. Enjoy Tony Bennett's unforgettable performance of “It Don't Mean A Thing (If It Ain't Got That Swing)” at London's majestic Royal Albert Hall in 1974. Let the haunting beauty of Nat King Cole's 1957 performance of “Autumn Leaves” and Louis Armstrong's performance of “Basin Street Blues” from 1959 delight you. Dean Martin's smooth vocal stylings will enthrall you as he performs the jazz standard “Wrap Your Troubles In Dreams". Witness the electrifying energy of Sammy Davis Jr.'s performance of “Too Close For Comfort”. Join us for this unforgettable musical experience that you'll surely cherish!

programme.title: Woody Herman, Paul Fontaine, Billy Hunt & Bill Chase
programme.sub-title: Woody Herman's Swinging Herd live in England 1964
programme.desc: Woody Herman: Live in '64 features a blistering one-hour concert from 1964 showcasing one of the very hottest line-ups of the "Swinging Herd," including trumpeter Bill Chase, trombonist Phil Wilson, and the amazing saxophonist Sal Nistico on the front line, as well as drummer Jake Hanna, bassist Chuck Andrus, and pianist-arranger Nat Pierce in the rhythm section. The band roars throughout the entire show which includes standards such as "Lonesome Old Town", "After You've Gone", and new originals including Charles Mingus's "Better Git It In Your Soul."

programme.title: Cannonball Adderley
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Avishai Cohen
programme.desc: Double bassist Avishai Cohen's trio with Noam David (drums) and Omri Mor (pianist) joined forces with the international Symphony Orchestra ‘INSO-Lviv', conducted by Christian Schumann. On June 24, 2017 they brought Avishai Cohen's signature blend of influences from Eastern Europe, American jazz and the Middle East to the Alfa Jazz Festival in Lviv, Ukraine. Cohen, who also supplies vocalizations in the Judeo-Spanish dialect Ladino, came to prominence internationally in the 1990s when jazz great Chick Corea offered him a place in his trio and a recording deal. Thirty years later, Cohen has become a house-hold name himself, leaving his Ukrainian audience enthralled and connected through the universal language of music.

programme.title: Coleman Hawkins
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Gary Burton & Makoto Ozone
programme.desc: 'Münchner Klaviersommer' was an annual concerts series that took place from 1981 to 1998 in Munich, Germany. Although the festival's name suggests a strong focus on piano music, it featured countless famous musicians from jazz and classical music – not just pianists. The concerts were usually held in July at The Gasteig, home of the Munich Philharmonic. In 1995, vibraphonist Gary Burton and pianist Makoto Ozone, both great jazz players noted for their virtuoso technique and innovative style, came together to give a concert of improvised music. They delighted the audience with their fluid, poetic artistry, which was expressed in a performance of the highest order.

programme.title: Wes Montgomery
programme.desc: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential jazz guitarists in history showcases these legends' unique essence. Revel in Wes Montgomery's virtuosity as he performs the captivating “Nica's Dream” in Holland in 1965, and delight in the expressive beauty of his rendition of “Here's That Rainy Day”, recorded in London in that same year. Experience the timeless appeal of Joe Pass's “You Stepped Out of a Dream” and “You Turned The Tables on Me”, recorded live in Hanover in 1975. The interplay between Jim Hall and Atilla Zoller borders on the impossible as they perform “Extensions” in Hanover in 1973. Enjoy Jim Hall's lyrical solo performance of “I'm Getting Sentimental Over You” from 1964. Witness Sonny Sharrock's avant-garde fusion of jazz and rock in an exhilarating performance of “Peanut", recorded live in France in 1970. Sharrock is joined by flutist Herbie Mann in a soulful performance of “Memphis Underground” from 1969. Immerse yourself in the magic of jazz guitar!

programme.title: Paco de Lucía
programme.sub-title: Germeringer Jazztage: Paco de Lucía & Group
programme.desc: This broadcast shows a rare appearance at the 1996 Germeringer Jazztage by the legendary flamenco guitarist Paco de Lucía and his sextet. Some may question the inclusion of a flamenco guitarist within a jazz festival. However, the similarities between flamenco and blues have been well documented: both are the outlet for a poor, disenfranchised minority, with a primitive strength, boundless capabilities for improvisation and a requirement for breathtaking virtuosity - all qualities shown by Paco de Lucía in this performance. Moreover, he has long been experimenting with jazz forms (evident even from his inclusion of bass, drums, and saxophone in his sextet), while still retaining the essence of the flamenco tradition. In his own words: “What I have tried to do is have a hand holding onto tradition and the other scratching, digging in other places trying to find new things I can bring into flamenco”.

programme.title: Herbert Blomstedt & Gewandhausorchester Leipzig
programme.sub-title: Symphonies in D by Mozart and Voríšek
programme.desc: Conductor Herbert Blomstedt and the Gewandhausorchester take us on a musical journey to the Czech Republic in this 2020 concert from Leipzig's Gewandhaus. The program opens with Bohemian composer Jan Václav Hugo Voríšek's Symphony in D major, Op. 23 (1821). Although the composition – Voríšek's only symphony – was never performed during the composer's lifetime, it has become one of his most-performed works today. Also on the program is Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Symphony No. 38 in D major, K. 504. This work, which premiered while Mozart was visiting Prague in 1787, is often referred to as the ‘Prague Symphony'.

programme.title: Seong-Jin Cho
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Ivan Fischer, Budapest Festival Orchestra & Richard Goode
programme.sub-title: Beethoven - Symphonies and Piano Concerto No. 4
programme.desc: Iván Fischer leads his Budapest Festival Orchestra (BFO) in a concert program dedicated to the music of Ludwig van Beethoven. The program opens with the composer's Symphony No. 1 in C major, Op. 21. Afterward, Fischer conducts Beethoven's Piano Concerto No. 4 in G major, Op. 58, in which Richard Goode features as the soloist. The acclaimed American pianist (1943) is known for his interpretations of the Beethoven repertoire. Goode recorded all five Beethoven piano concertos with the BFO and Fischer, earning him exceptional critical acclaim and a Grammy Award nomination. As an encore, Goode performs the Sarabande from J. S. Bach's Partita in B-flat major, BWV 825. The program closes with Beethoven's famous Symphony No. 5 in C minor, Op. 67. This performance was recorded at the Béla Bartók National Concert Hall in Budapest, Hungary, on January 29, 2017.

programme.title: Les Dissonances
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Mademoiselle Karen, Jakob Munch, Danish National Symphony Orchestra & Emma Smith
programme.desc: One night only! Welcome to the spectacular and decadent world of The Babylon Hotel, where music pours out of every crevice like bubbling champagne. In this concert, the upper class meets the underworld in a melting pot of euphoria and extravagance, nostalgia and pleasure with a carefree sinfulness only seen in the exuberant nightlife of the 1920s around the world. The concert features iconic music from movies and series, such as The Great Gatsby, Burlesque, and Babylon Berlin. The music is performed by the Danish National Symphony Orchestra and DR Big Band under the direction of Miho Hazama. The concert also features the Moka Efti Orchestra, and singers Madame le Pustra, Emma Smith, Mademoiselle Karen, Jakob Munch, and Nikko Weidemann, as well as dancers of Sweet Burlesque. This performance was recorded at the DR Koncerthuset in Copenhagen, Denmark, in May 2023.

programme.title: Ludwig Wicki, Danish National Symphony Orchestra, DR Junior Choir & Andrea Lykke Oehlenschlæger
programme.desc: Swiss conductor Ludwig Wicki leads the Danish National Symphony Orchestra and the DR Junior Choir in a sparkling concert program of film music. The program features a selection of music from great and Oscar-winning Hollywood films, including Titanic, The Bodyguard, Pirates of the Caribbean, Braveheart, and Moulin Rouge, written by major composers such as John Williams, Hans Zimmer, James Horner, and Klaus Badelt. Highlights include ‘He's a pirate/Davy Jones' from Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest ‘Now We Are Free' from Gladiator ‘My Heart Will Go On' from Titanic and ‘Shallow' from A Star Is Born. Featured soloists in this concert are the young talented singers Andrea Lykke Oehlenschlæger and Diluckshan Jeyaratnam. This performance was recorded at the DR Koncerthuset in Copenhagen, Denmark, in May 2022.

programme.title: Iggy Pop
programme.desc: Iggy Pop is the vocalist of influential proto-punk band The Stooges, who reunited in 2003, and has been known for his outrageous and unpredictable stage antics. Pop's music has encompassed a number of styles over the course of his career, including garage rock, hard rock, new wave, jazz, art rock and bluesIn 2010, The Stooges were inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.Journalists and critics discuss the rise of Iggy.

programme.title: Flying Over Bermuda

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland 2
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland II: Summer in the Golden Circle" presents a serene aerial view of Iceland's south coast, starting at Skogafoss and moving to Vik, with black sand beaches, cliffs, and Vatnajökull glacier. Filmed in 2020, this 1-hour film features local wildlife and immersive music.

programme.title: Flying Over Kauai
programme.desc: "Flying Over Kauai" provides a soothing aerial journey over Hawaii's Garden Island, featuring the lush NaPali Coast, waterfalls, canyons, and beaches. Paired with new music by The Cynic Project and nature sounds, this 4K film offers a visually and sonically immersive experience.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.
